report_metadata.title: Kensington Avenue Facade Analysis for 3D Reconstruction
directories: PHOTOS KENSINGTON sorted/Kensington Ave _ Baldwin St/
PHOTOS KENSINGTON sorted/Kensington Ave _ Dundas St W/
report_metadata.notes: Most photos taken at night under artificial lighting. Colour descriptions may be affected by sodium vapour / LED streetlight colour cast. Many images are rotated 90 degrees (phone held landscape). Kensington Ave runs roughly north-south between Dundas St W and Baldwin St in the Kensington Market HCD, Toronto.

IMG_20260317_124549726_HDR.jpg.probable_address: Kensington Ave near Dundas St W (south end, west side)
IMG_20260317_124549726_HDR.jpg.brick_colour: yellow/buff brick visible at left; red brick at upper right on adjacent building

windows.upper_floor: not clearly visible behind mural

IMG_20260317_124549726_HDR.jpg.roof_type: flat with parapet (main building); gable peak visible on adjacent red brick building at left
IMG_20260317_124549726_HDR.jpg.decorative_elements: large-scale community mural covering most of facade; text reads 'Kensington Community' and references kensingtonmarket.to; yellow banner section
IMG_20260317_124549726_HDR.jpg.storefront_details: ground floor entirely covered with murals and street art; appears to be a shuttered/boarded commercial space with extensive graffiti and painted murals including bee imagery
IMG_20260317_124549726_HDR.jpg.condition: fair to poor; facade heavily covered with murals and graffiti; underlying structure obscured
IMG_20260317_124549726_HDR.jpg.signage: Community mural text: 'Kensington Community United'; kensingtonmarket.to website reference
IMG_20260317_124549726_HDR.jpg.party_walls: party wall visible at left (red brick building adjoining); party wall at right

colour_palette.accent: pink, green, teal from street art

IMG_20260320_011827369_HDR.jpg.probable_address: Kensington Ave (interior/covered market passage or loading area)
IMG_20260320_011827369_HDR.jpg.description: Interior shot of a covered space, possibly a market loading dock or covered passage behind Kensington Ave buildings. Pressed tin/metal ceiling visible. Chain-link fencing in foreground. Dimly lit with scattered ground-level lights. Stickers on metal fence posts. Not a building facade - this appears to be a service/loading area behind the storefronts.
IMG_20260320_011827369_HDR.jpg.condition: utilitarian; stickers and wear on fixtures

IMG_20260320_012006796_HDR.jpg.probable_address: Kensington Ave (west side, near Dundas St W end)
IMG_20260320_012006796_HDR.jpg.facade_material: mixed: corrugated metal siding (green, lower left), board-and-batten or vertical siding (lower centre), brick (upper right section)
IMG_20260320_012006796_HDR.jpg.brick_colour: red-brown brick on upper storey right section

windows.upper_floor: 3 windows visible across facade; 1 small square window (left, in green metal section), 1 large window (centre, in brick section), 1 medium window (right, in brick section with white frame)

IMG_20260320_012006796_HDR.jpg.doors: 1 central entrance (appears to be a small vestibule/booth painted teal/green); 1 door at left (white, residential-style); storefront openings at right
IMG_20260320_012006796_HDR.jpg.roof_type: flat with low parapet; metal/wood fascia
IMG_20260320_012006796_HDR.jpg.storefront_details: ground floor has picnic bench seating behind wooden fence/railing; patio area; appears to be a restaurant or bar; corrugated metal and wood siding at ground level
IMG_20260320_012006796_HDR.jpg.condition: fair; eclectic mix of materials suggests multiple renovations; weathered wood fence
IMG_20260320_012006796_HDR.jpg.signage: not clearly legible in night photo

IMG_20260320_012017528_HDR.jpg.probable_address: Kensington Ave near Dundas St W (west side), possibly around 2-8 Kensington Ave

windows.gable_attic: 1 window per gable peak (3 gables visible), small rectangular or pointed windows
windows.upper_floor: 3 windows visible across the row; mix of single-hung and casement; some with white frames, some with blinds

IMG_20260320_012017528_HDR.jpg.doors: ground floor commercial entrances: 1 glass double door (left building), 1 entrance with awning (right building)
IMG_20260320_012017528_HDR.jpg.roof_type: gable (bay-and-gable typology); 3 prominent front-facing gables in a row; steep pitch approximately 45 degrees
IMG_20260320_012017528_HDR.jpg.decorative_elements: prominent bay-and-gable Victorian form; decorative gable trim/bargeboard; pointed gable peaks; likely original ornamental woodwork at gable apex
IMG_20260320_012017528_HDR.jpg.storefront_details: left unit: glass door commercial entrance with patio fence; centre unit: Chinese signage with red and gold elements (possible restaurant); right unit: commercial with awning and signage in Chinese characters
IMG_20260320_012017528_HDR.jpg.condition: fair; some weathering; original Victorian form well-preserved above storefronts; ground floor heavily modified for commercial use
IMG_20260320_012017528_HDR.jpg.signage: Chinese characters visible on centre and right storefronts; one reads similar to a hair salon or restaurant
IMG_20260320_012017528_HDR.jpg.party_walls: row housing - party walls on both sides of each unit; continuous row of at least 3 bay-and-gable houses

IMG_20260320_012031164_HDR.jpg.probable_address: Kensington Ave (west side), near Dundas end
IMG_20260320_012031164_HDR.jpg.facade_material: mixed: painted brick/stucco (left), painted brick (centre, grey/blue), brick (right, partially visible)

IMG_20260320_012031164_HDR.jpg.bond_pattern: obscured by paint on most surfaces

windows.upper_floor: centre building: 1 pointed-arch or narrow window in gable, 2 windows on second floor with white trim; left building: 1 large window; right building: partially visible
windows.ground_floor: centre building has blue-lit commercial space (barber shop with blue LED lighting and barber pole); left building has commercial space with graffiti on side

IMG_20260320_012031164_HDR.jpg.doors: centre: single commercial entrance with steps up; left: rear/side access visible

IMG_20260320_012031164_HDR.jpg.decorative_elements: centre building: pointed gable with decorative trim; bay window form at second floor; barber pole; white trim around windows and gable edges
IMG_20260320_012031164_HDR.jpg.storefront_details: centre: barber shop with prominent blue LED interior lighting; blue neon glow visible through windows; left: appears shuttered with graffiti
IMG_20260320_012031164_HDR.jpg.condition: centre: good (recently renovated/painted); left: poor (heavy graffiti on dumpster/side wall); right: fair
IMG_20260320_012031164_HDR.jpg.signage: barber pole on centre building; graffiti tags on left building infrastructure
IMG_20260320_012031164_HDR.jpg.party_walls: party walls both sides of centre building

IMG_20260320_012138541_HDR.jpg.probable_address: Kensington Ave (east side), streetscape view looking north
IMG_20260320_012138541_HDR.jpg.description: Wide streetscape view showing the east side of Kensington Ave. Multiple bay-and-gable Victorian rowhouses visible in a continuous row. Red-brown brick facades with steep gables. Street-level commercial uses. Street light glare partially obscures details. A vehicle and tree are in the foreground. At least 4-5 buildings visible in the row.
IMG_20260320_012138541_HDR.jpg.condition: mixed; typical Kensington Market mix of maintained and weathered facades

IMG_20260320_012706597_HDR.jpg.condition: fair; mural panels in good condition; some weathering

IMG_20260320_012716439_HDR.jpg.condition: good (window and frame in good condition)
IMG_20260320_012716439_HDR.jpg.notes: Close-up only - need wider shot for full facade reconstruction

IMG_20260320_013016711_HDR.jpg.description: Wider view of the Lost Gen Vintage corner building showing both the Kensington Ave facade and the cross-street (St Andrew St) facade. Extensive red/orange mural artwork wraps around the corner at ground level. Above, the facade is painted stucco/render in cream/white with graffiti. Behind the corner building, the continuous row of bay-and-gable Victorian houses is visible along Kensington Ave, with their characteristic steep gables and red-brown brick. A lavender/purple painted building is visible in the middle distance.
IMG_20260320_013016711_HDR.jpg.condition: fair; murals in good condition; upper walls show graffiti and some deterioration

common_decorative_elements: bay window projections at second floor
decorative diamond/lozenge brickwork patterns (buff brick buildings)
summary.storefront_character: heavily modified ground floors with diverse commercial uses: vintage shops, food shops, restaurants, barber shops, cannabis shops, leather goods, cheese shops, gift shops
notable_signage: Still Trader / Buy Me / Lost Boys

colour_palette_summary.common_paint_colours: grey-blue, green, pink, white, cream (various painted-over facades)
colour_palette_summary.signage_colours: highly varied - yellow, blue, red, pink, black, neon blue/purple

summary.street_character: Kensington Ave is a narrow north-south street lined predominantly with 2.5-storey Victorian bay-and-gable rowhouses converted to ground-floor commercial with residential above. The street has heavy mural/street art coverage, eclectic commercial signage, and a bohemian market atmosphere. Buildings date primarily from the 1889-1913 period. Most original facades are well-preserved above the storefront level, with ground floors heavily modified for retail use. Party walls are continuous throughout.
photo_limitations: Most photos taken at night - colour accuracy affected by artificial lighting (sodium vapour orange cast, LED blue/white cast)
Many photos rotated 90 degrees requiring mental rotation
Some facades obscured by trees, vehicles, or signage
Interior/rear photos not useful for facade reconstruction
Night photography limits assessment of brick condition, mortar joints, and subtle decorative details
recommendations_for_3d: Use PXL_20260318_223334974.jpg (daytime Love Calista photo) as colour calibration reference
Bay-and-gable typology with steep front-facing gable is the default template for most buildings
Assume running bond brick pattern throughout unless clearly different
Ground floor storefronts should be modelled with large display windows and central or offset entrances
Party walls are continuous - no side windows on most buildings
Decorative diamond brick patterns are common on buff/yellow brick buildings
White-painted bargeboard at gable peaks is nearly universal
Expect 2-3 windows on upper floor per building width
Consider separate daytime photo survey for accurate colour and detail capture
